subject: [PATCH v3 1/2] mm/vmpressure: skip tree=true accounting on cgroup v2

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the early return condition could be simplified to a single line using a ternary operator, and suggested this as an alternative

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledges a mistake in their previous patch, proposes a fix and asks for application or re-spinning of the revised patch.

The author is addressing a concern about the complexity of the guard condition in vmpressure(). They simplified the condition by using the equality operator (==) instead of the logical OR operator, which makes it clear that both cgroup_subsys_on_dfl() and tree are bool values. The author agreed to restructure the condition in this way.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah sorry about this! I just amended my last patch to move code
from vmpressure-v1.c to memcontrol-v1.c and just sent it, without
other changes. Forgot Shakeels ack on v2 as well :(

Andrew would you mind applying the below fixlet? I can also respin
if its easier. Thanks!!

---

From: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 09:25:05 -0700
Subject: [fixlet] mm/vmpressure: skip tree=true accounting on cgroup v2

Simplify the guard. Both cgroup_subsys_on_dfl() and tree are bool, so
the two combinations that have no consumer (v1 + tree=false, v2 +
tree=true) are exactly the cases where dfl == tree.

Suggested-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>
Signed-off-by: Usama Arif <usama.arif@linux.dev>
---
 mm/vmpressure.c | 3 +--
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/mm/vmpressure.c b/mm/vmpressure.c
index 14470141bbe6..9629240d77ad 100644
--- a/mm/vmpressure.c
+++ b/mm/vmpressure.c
@@ -120,8 +120,7 @@ void vmpressure(gfp_t gfp, int order, struct mem_cgroup *memcg, bool tree,
 	 *   cgroup v1 + tree=true  -> userspace eventfds (memory.pressure_level)
 	 * Skip the other two: nothing consumes the result.
 	 */
-	if ((!cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) && !tree) ||
-	    (cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) && tree))
+	if (cgroup_subsys_on_dfl(memory_cgrp_subsys) == tree)
 		return;
 
 	vmpr = memcg_to_vmpressure(memcg);
-- 
2.53.0-Meta

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds an early return to the vmpressure function in the Linux kernel, specifically for cgroup v2 and tree=true scenarios. In these cases, the function would previously perform unnecessary work, including lock contention, which could serialize reclaimers on a single global sr_lock. The patch eliminates this unnecessary work by skipping the cgroup v2 + tree = true case, reducing the number of calls to vmpressure() and improving performance on large systems with many memcgs.
